name: Karl Dykema
title: Project Lead / Landowner
bio: Karl owns the 40-acre oak-pine barrens parcel in Newaygo County and has been working toward its restoration for several years. He leads the day-to-day project work, coordinating the burn program, invasive removal, and species monitoring.

name: Emily Guyot
title: LSFSC Intern
bio: Emily is a 2026 intern with the Lake States Fire Science Consortium, supporting botanical surveys, lupine monitoring transects, and field data collection on the project. She holds NWCG Firefighter Type 2 (FFT2) certification, qualifying her to serve on wildland and prescribed fire crews under agency supervision.

name: Rod Denning
title: District Forester
org: Newaygo Conservation District / Michigan Forestry Assistance Program
bio: Rod is the District Forester serving Muskegon, Newaygo, and Oceana counties through the Michigan Forestry Assistance Program (MDARD). He holds a B.S. in Forestry from Michigan State University and an M.A. in Geography from Western Michigan University, and is both a GIS specialist and certified arborist. He provides forestry technical assistance and guidance to the project.

name: Stuart Goldman
title: Conservation Coordinator, Western Lower Peninsula
bio: Stuart is a Conservation Coordinator for the Western Lower Peninsula at the Michigan Nature Association (MNA), bringing more than two decades of stewardship and ecological land management experience. He advises the project on restoration strategy and connects it to MNA's broader conservation network in west Michigan.

name: Jacob Lemon
title: Monitoring & Community Science Manager
bio: Jake is Trout Unlimited's Monitoring and Community Science Manager, supporting research, monitoring, and inventory efforts across Michigan and Wisconsin. Based in Rockford, MI, he brings expertise in biological monitoring, community science program design, and data collection — skills directly applicable to the species and habitat tracking work on this project.

name: Tyler Bassett
title: Conservation Associate — Botanist
bio: Tyler is a botanist and plant ecologist with the Michigan Natural Features Inventory at MSU Extension. He studies the ecology, classification, and management of natural communities with a focus on the rare plant species they support, and has a particular passion for the fire-suppressed prairie-savanna landscapes of the upper Midwest. He holds a Ph.D. in Ecology, Evolutionary Biology, and Behavior from Michigan State University.
